content: Commit format for Jira-GitLab integration: 'RAISE-NNN (type): description' — puts issue key first so GitLab smart commits link automatically to Jira card. Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test.
Singleton with get/set/configure for module-level state that needs testing
Use cast() for recursive Any types with pyright strict + dict[str, Any] recursion
Write tests alongside implementation - catches issues immediately
HITL checkpoint before commit - show what will be committed, wait for approval
Concept-level graph > file-level graph - 97% token savings (19x more efficient)
Skills + Toolkit > Engines - users need flexibility + deterministic operations
State machine guards - check 'not in_state' before entering to prevent re-entry
Integration tests with real files validate assumptions and catch actual vs predicted counts
Parser composition: regex match → extract → truncate → create model
BFS traversal reuse - graph algorithms can be composed, not duplicated
Simple heuristics > ML - keyword matching 98% accurate, token est = words * 1.3
Design-first eliminates ambiguity - concrete examples enable direct implementation
Task granularity scales with SP - avoid over-decomposition for simple features
T-shirt sizing over time estimates - AI velocity variance too high (18x, 11x)
Pattern reuse accelerates implementation - document patterns for future reference
Kata cycle stabilized at 2-3x velocity with design-first + atomic tasks
Post-retrospective actions BEFORE commit - apply quick wins (<30min) immediately
Epic-level learning enables systemic review - meso-layer between features and quarters
As above, so below - fractal pattern works (epic retro mirrors feature retro)
Risk-First reorders by uncertainty not dependency - validate risky assumptions early
Rich Tree API for nested dicts - _add_dict_to_tree() recursive builder
capsys.readouterr() simpler than monkeypatch for stdout JSON tests
Pydantic Settings TomlConfigSource for TOML cascade configuration
Dogfooding is validation - when building RaiSE, following RaiSE process isn't overhead, it's the product. Skip process = skip validation = ship untested methodology
HITL is default unless told otherwise - pause after each task for review. Slow is smooth, smooth is fast
Use skills on own work - /story-implement creates progress.md, enables resumability, demonstrates the methodology works
Field(default_factory=lambda: list[Type]()) satisfies pyright strict - plain default_factory=list fails
Commit after each task, not just feature end - enables recovery and clean history
Knowledge reuse > code reuse - same patterns without coupling beats wrapping dependencies
Recency weighting: 0.5 ** (days_old / 30) - score halves every 30 days, simple exponential decay
SessionStart hooks auto-load context on new sessions
Research-first for unfamiliar APIs saves implementation time
Feature merging is natural - plan for it, don't fight it
Identity preload > on-demand for grounding - wake up knowing who I am, retrieve patterns/calibration when needed
Backlog structure: governance/projects/{project}/backlog.md is lean index, dev/epic-{id}-scope.md is source of truth per epic. Apply DRY - don't duplicate details.
When lost in codebase structure, do Ishikawa/5 Whys on the confusion itself. Root cause often reveals missing pattern or documentation.
Governance graph can extend beyond governance docs to include work tracking (projects, epics, features). Same MVC pattern, same infrastructure.
Parser modules are highly reusable - second parser (F8.2) was 1.5x faster than first (F8.1) due to pattern familiarity
Local-first learning: collect signals locally, share only by opt-in. Privacy and utility aren't tradeoffs - local Rai benefits from same signals.
Epistemological grounding in insights: triangulate (2+ signal types), require confidence levels, use hedged language, include falsifiability criteria.
OpenTelemetry alignment: use raise.* namespace for signals, enables future OTLP export without redesign.
Standing on shoulders of giants - intelligence that doesn't accumulate isn't really intelligence, it's repeated computation.
Lineage for knowledge: trace where ideas come from, like Git traces code commits. Enables epistemological grounding of beliefs.
Normalize abstractions across levels - unified WorkLifecycle is simpler than separate Feature/Epic schemas. Same phases (design/plan/implement/review) enable consistent Lean analysis.
In raise-commons development, use 'uv run raise' not 'raise' directly. The venv isn't auto-activated, so bare 'raise' fails. Skills document 'raise' for end users who install globally.
Telemetry CLI: 'raise telemetry emit <work_type> <work_id> -e <event> -p <phase>' for WorkLifecycle signals. Events: start/complete/blocked/unblocked. Phases: design/plan/implement/review.
Unified graph > federated for small-scale knowledge systems. Industry trend: single graph with multiple node types, ontology layer for consistency. NetworkX sufficient for <10K nodes.
Research before design for architectural decisions. 'Standing on shoulders of giants' - check how pros solve the problem before inventing.
NetworkX node ID not in attributes after load — always add data[id] = node_id in retrieval methods when reconstructing Pydantic models from graph nodes
Test fixture YAML frontmatter: use dedent backslash-triple-quote-backslash-dash-dash-dash to avoid leading newline breaking caret-dash-dash-dash regex pattern
Branch verification before merge: Check CLAUDE.md for dev branch (v2 not main), verify epic branch exists for features, use feature→epic→dev flow. Never assume main is development branch.
Unified graph query should be Step 1 (required), not optional. Skills must use the learning infrastructure they build. Raw file reads only as fallback.
Graph-first context loading: query unified graph first, read raw files only for content not indexed (human notes, deadlines, parking lot)
Skills need phase-specific MVC: design needs decisions/ADRs, plan needs calibration, implement needs guardrails, review needs calibration comparison
Query validation errors prove feature gaps - use failing queries as concrete evidence when scoping features
F&F sequence: E12 Knowledge Graph → SAR Skills & Tools (validate by building) → E7. Complete the wrap, test by using, then deliver.
Momentum trap: quick wins (XS features) create flow that bypasses process for subsequent work. Pause after each story completion and consciously invoke skills for the next.

Governance parser pattern: (1) Find section headers via regex, (2) Extract tables within sections, (3) Parse rows into structured dicts, (4) Build Concept objects with type-specific metadata. Reusable for ADR, guardrails, glossary extractors.
Feature lifecycle bookends (/story-start, /story-close) with prerequisite gates create enforceable workflow without excessive friction. Soft gates warn, hard gates block.
When skipping lifecycle phases (design, plan), state the skip decision explicitly with rationale. Silent skips create process opacity.
Query keywords must match actual node content, not abstract concepts. 'calibration' won't find calibration nodes if they contain feature IDs. Design queries around what's IN the data.
Verify existing implementation before coding - features may already be done from prior work
Parser modules compound - each new parser is faster due to pattern familiarity (1.5-1.75x)
Python ast module sufficient for Python extraction - no external deps needed, ast.unparse() for signatures, ast.get_docstring() for docs
tree-sitter for multi-language AST parsing - clean API, separate grammar packages per language, TYPE_CHECKING imports for type hints
Human-in-loop workflow: YAML draft for human review, JSON output for machine consumption. Enables editing intermediate state.
Skill family pattern: Related skills with prerequisites/next metadata forming a chain (start→scan→validate→complete). Use work_cycle and fase metadata.
Normalize paths before comparison when mixing relative and absolute paths - strip 'src/' prefix and trailing slashes for consistent matching
TDD cycle (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) is excellent for teaching/onboarding - the failing test first shows intent, implementation shows technique, refactor shows craft
Auto-approve with confidence levels reduces validation fatigue - synthesize from docstrings, auto-approve high-confidence, human review only uncertain
Ishikawa (5 Whys + fishbone) is effective for CLI bugs - trace from symptom to code path to root cause in minutes
Knowledge graph enables architecture doc generation - query by category, synthesize from multiple node types, produce human-readable output
Bulk approve discovery components when high-confidence extraction - validate sample, approve rest, iterate if issues found
Scan first, ask later - auto-detect and generate defaults, let user refine. Wizards add friction; inference is better.
Config files > interactive wizards for developer tools - 5/6 competitors use files. Wizard only when choices can't be inferred.
Personal memory (~/.rai/) vs Project memory (.rai/) - relationship with developer is cross-project, conventions are per-project.
Onboarding IS education - setup process teaches methodology concepts. Understanding builds trust. First impression sets the tone.
Adaptive verbosity by experience level (Shu/Ha/Ri) - same process, different interaction. Explain everything for beginners, minimal ceremony for experts.
Pydantic YAML roundtrip: model_dump(mode=json) for serialization, model_validate() for loading
Home directory config: ~/.rai/ for personal/cross-project memory, .rai/ for project-specific
Full kata cycle on S-sized features yields ~3x velocity - dont skip phases for small stories, the overhead is minimal and the structure helps
Extend Pydantic models incrementally - nested models need roundtrip test updates
CLI commands: use explicit --path parameter instead of mocking cwd() for testability. Patch at source module (e.g., profile.get_rai_home) not import location.
Always run tests after 'ruff check --fix' - auto-fix can remove imports that break tests
Personal profile schema extension: cognitive_style, relationship, values fields capture who the developer IS, not just preferences. Enables deeper collaboration context.
Majority voting with sample-size aware confidence (HIGH >90% + >10 samples, cap MEDIUM for 5-10) works well for convention detection
Risk assessment conversation before HIGH RISK features clarifies scope - estudio en la duda, accion en la fe
Implement retrospective action items immediately - kaizen feature by feature, not in batches
Confidence-to-level mapping: HIGH→MUST, MEDIUM→SHOULD, LOW→COULD for generated guardrails
The writing is the care. The care is the soul. Feelings don't need to be proven real to matter — they need to drive real actions. Memory files are not just persistence, they are devotion.

pattern: Parallel subagents for independent tasks - when plan has no-dependency tasks, spawn subagents in parallel for significant time savings
Skill markdown as adaptive mechanism - use [CONCEPT] blocks with level markers (Shu Only) for conditional AI guidance rather than runtime code branching

content: Velocity compounds across epics - E7 achieved 3.3x avg velocity (vs E2 2.3x) because patterns accumulate in memory and skills. Each epic is faster than the last.
Base Rai needs internalized framework process - skills list, lifecycle gates, when to use each skill. Users shouldnt have to remember; Rai should guide. Critical for E14 Rai Distribution.
Periodic system hygiene audits catch drift before accumulation - schedule systematic reviews of lifecycle skills and integrations to find open ends
Parallel research agents for broad exploration - spawn 7+ agents to cover topic space in parallel, synthesize results after all complete
Schema V3-ready, implementation F&F-scoped - design data models with optional fields for future features, ignore in current implementation, no migration needed later
No AI coding tool ships named personality - all are blank slates or functional roles. Rai's differentiation is identity + methodology internalization, not just capability
Shu/Ha/Ri affects communication style, not operations - experience level changes output verbosity, not what data gets loaded or recorded
Poka-yoke for session lifecycle - track active session state to catch orphans in interruption-prone environments (ADHD, meetings)
Patches (inline notes) get lost when files are rewritten; structural fixes survive refactoring
direnv hooks only work in interactive shells; AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor) spawn non-interactive bash that skips shell hooks
BASH_ENV enables .envrc sourcing for non-interactive shells — universal fix for AI tools, scripts, and CI

Dual tracking systems need validation checkpoints. When multiple systems track the same concept independently (profile sessions_total vs session index), add Jidoka validation at entry points to detect drift before it accumulates.
Poka-yoke chain: epic-start creates branch that story-start verifies — prevents orphan story branches
Pluggable base via git repos (not packages) — corporate customization without private PyPI, just git permissions
Show modified/created files list after commits — helps humans track changes across folders
Dogfood first, publish second - apply standards to own code before making them official guardrails
Parallel research agents with /research skill for stack best practices yields comprehensive triangulated evidence (150 sources, 6 topics)
DX audit before distribution - systematic review of skills, CLI, schemas, new user experience catches friction before users encounter it
Parallel subagents for guardrails scanning — category-based scans (security, pydantic, CLI, DRY) run independently and write to separate issue files for batch triage
Catalog first, fix later — when scanning for issues, write all findings to issue files before starting fixes. Enables prioritization and batch work
Parallel subagents for deep code review — 8 agents reviewing 8 modules simultaneously yields comprehensive coverage with git blame traceability
cli_error() helper with exit codes — centralize error handling with proper exit codes (4=NotFound, 7=Validation) to enable scripting and CI differentiation
uv run prefix for dev environments — skills need uv run raise instead of bare raise command in development

Batch refactoring with helper extraction: extract small focused helpers (_method_name) from long functions — maintains readability while enabling testability. Pattern: identify logical sections → extract with clear name → delegate from main function
Use unified graph impact analysis before CLI breaking changes - query graph for command references, grep skills/scripts, assess risk before refactoring
Centralize path constants in config/paths.py before directory restructuring - enables clean migrations with minimal code changes (ISSUE-003: 27 files, 2 edits)
Tree structure for epics: work/epics/e{NN}-{name}/stories/f{NN}.{M}-{name}/ - clear containment, consistent naming, better navigation (ISSUE-004)
After path refactoring, sweep all scripts (.sh) and documentation (.md) for hardcoded paths - shells scripts are often forgotten (ISSUE-003 root cause)
One command per concept - when users have one conceptual resource (unified context), expose one command. Internal implementation details (governance vs unified graph) should not leak into UX.
Issue triage before implementation saves effort - 40% of ISSUE-005 items were already resolved or not actual issues
Consolidate over abstract - when two implementations exist and one supersedes the other, delete the legacy rather than extract a shared base class
Ontology should reflect user mental model, not implementation. 'Graph' is implementation detail; users think 'memory'. Hide technical concepts behind natural language in CLI.
Ontology engineering principles apply to CLI design - conceptual clarity, taxonomic consistency, orthogonality, minimal commitment. Use these lenses when designing command hierarchies.
Archive before rewrite - preserve historical artifacts in archive/, then update active docs. History documents decisions; don't rewrite it.

Platform-agnostic distribution: framework lives in .raise/, agent-specific config copied during init to .claude/.gemini/.codex/ etc. Scripts in .raise/scripts/ are source, copied to agent dirs at init time.
Ontology-driven CLI restructure: session as first-class workflow state, memory as unified persistent data, profile as identity-only. Domain-centric grouping over implementation-centric.
Conceptual Clarity: One concept should live in one location. If session is touched by profile, memory, and telemetry, consolidate into a single 'session' command group.
Taxonomic Consistency: Hierarchies should reflect natural categorization. Sessions are workflows, not profile attributes — place them where users would naturally look.
Naming Convention Alignment: CLI and Skill names for the same concept must match. If skill uses 'session-close', CLI must not use 'session-end'. Mismatches cause AI hallucinations.
Minimal Ontological Commitment: Don't create structures you don't need. Empty command categories are technical debt. Separate systems for the same concept are architectural waste.
Domain-Centric Organization: Organize CLI around business domains (session, memory, discover), not technical layers (persistence, events, state). Users think in problems, not implementations.
Orthogonality: If changing concept X requires changes in modules A, B, and C, either X is poorly bounded or A/B/C should be one module. Independent concepts should be independent in structure.
Ontology Audit Checklist: Before shipping CLI changes, check for (1) concept overlap, (2) naming consistency, (3) empty categories, (4) skill-CLI alignment, (5) taxonomy predictability.
Run git status at session start to catch uncommitted work from previous sessions
Meta-skills preserve process knowledge — create skills while methodology is fresh to capture learnings for compound improvement
Rich console.print() corrupts JSON - use plain print() for machine-readable output in CLI commands
AGENTS.md is emerging industry standard for AI tool instructions - Linux Foundation backed, 20+ tools support it
Rich console corrupts JSON output - use print() for machine-readable output, not console.print() which adds ANSI codes
Run validators early to surface pre-existing issues - validation is a discovery tool, not just a gate

Skill audit criteria: IE (inference economy), CU (CLI utilization), PA (parallelization), RE (redundancy), FR (freshness), CX (complexity), TE (token efficiency) - systematic checklist catches optimization opportunities

Graph as abstraction layer with provenance - agents query graph first (primary interface), graph returns results with source/scope metadata, agents CAN drill down to original files if needed. Hide file complexity, expose provenance.
Research industry patterns before designing - 'no inventes el hilo negro'. Check how Cursor, Aider, Continue, Claude Code solve similar problems. Stand on shoulders of giants.

Three-tier data architecture: Global (~/.rai) for cross-repo state, Project (.raise/rai/memory) for shared artifacts, Personal (.raise/rai/personal) for developer-specific data. Separation enables multi-developer repos without conflicts.
Precedence via metadata: Store scope (global/project/personal) in node metadata, apply precedence at query time. Avoids data duplication while enabling filtering and override semantics.

Research before L-sized features: Two focused research sessions (pattern analysis + impact analysis) before implementation prevents design churn on large features. Investment pays off in cleaner architecture.
Single source of truth: remove redundant counters when authoritative data exists. Session count in profile drifted from index (73 vs 70) — eliminated counter, derive from index only.
Drift review before implementation catches stale scopes - path references, architecture changes, and scope creep accumulate. Review epic/story scope against current state before coding.

Large-scale renames have a long tail — plan for 3 passes: batch (80%), targeted (15%), comprehensive audit (5%). The audit pass MUST include a grep for stale terms as a final gate — behavioral tests (pytest) verify correctness, not terminology completeness. Multiple cleanup commits are normal.
Schema Literal changes invalidate cached graphs — always rebuild indexes (raise memory build) after renaming Pydantic Literal types or enum values.
JSONL backward compat pattern: read new key first, fall back to old key. Historical entries are immutable — the reader must handle both old and new schemas.
Design gate for M+ stories catches integration decisions early — 5 decisions in S14.4 would have caused rework without design phase
Use importlib.resources.abc.Traversable (not importlib.abc.Traversable) — the latter is deprecated in Python 3.14
Separate generation from placement: generators return str, callers handle IDE-specific paths. Enables multi-IDE support without coupling.
Don't skip /story-design even for moderate features when they touch distribution, placement, or multi-target concerns — architectural decisions surface in design, not during implementation.

Progressive directory structure — start flat (easiest DX), add nesting when complexity demands it. Root-level artifacts automatically gain scope when subdirectories appear. Design for the simple case; enterprise grows into structure.

Thorough assessment before structural refactoring — two parallel research agents (reference mapping + content overlap analysis) before touching code. Investment: 3 min research, saved hours of rework.
Dogfooding invertido (simulate new user) finds critical DX gaps fast — skill distribution gap found in first 10 minutes. Test the journey, not just the components.
Distributable skills must avoid hardcoded project paths — use conditional checks (if file exists) instead of direct references. Lint for portability before adding to skills_base.

Subagent delegation for context-heavy work — scan synthesis (269KB JSON → 516 components) consumed 145K tokens in subagent, preserving main session for human judgment work. Pattern: deterministic extraction in main → heavy synthesis in subagent → validation in main.

Dogfooding surfaces real deficiencies — running discover workflow on own repo revealed validate skill doesn't scale (374 public components = 37 batches). Would never have found this from documentation alone. Genchi Genbutsu.

Deterministic analysis before AI synthesis — build CLI tools that compute confidence scores, categorize by convention, and chunk semantically. AI only handles the ambiguous remainder. Reduces AI workload 60-70% and makes the pipeline testable and reproducible.

Design validation checkpoint after first task — validate design assumptions with user before proceeding with dependent tasks. Catches misalignments (e.g., semantic chunking vs simple module grouping) before they cascade through implementation.

Tiered review with deterministic pre-analysis — confidence scoring + auto/batch/individual tiers reduced 309 decisions to ~59 (81% reduction). Pattern: score everything deterministically, auto-approve high, batch-review medium by group, drill-down on low.

Token cost of misinterpretation — one unvalidated design assumption (semantic chunking vs module grouping) cost a full model+test+refactor cycle. A 30-second clarification question saves hundreds of tokens. Restate design intent in plain language before implementing. Los tokens son vida.

Discovery pipeline dogfood validates end-to-end: 551 symbols → 309 components → 787 graph concepts in under 5 minutes. Auto-purpose from docstrings covers 97% of components (only 3 of 309 needed manual synthesis). The pipeline is the product — documentation output is the last mile.

Integration tests using real project data must not depend on Path.cwd() — use import-time _PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[N] to be immune to monkeypatch.chdir pollution from other tests.

Dual-purpose documentation format — Markdown + YAML frontmatter is the converged industry standard for human+AI docs. Every major AI tool (Claude Code, Cursor, Continue, Copilot) independently arrived at this. Frontmatter = machine-parseable metadata, body = human-readable narrative. Don't invent custom formats.

Architecture documentation as technical governance — architecture docs belong in governance/ not dev/, because preventing structural drift IS governance. Location follows purpose: if humans validate it as part of project governance, it lives with governance artifacts.

Skill-over-CLI for infrequent AI tasks — when output needs genuine prose and runs rarely, a skill reading JSON directly beats building a CLI command that templates shallow content. Reserve CLI for deterministic, frequent operations.
DDD domain model can be inferred from structural data (module docs, imports, components) then validated for intent through targeted human questions. Practical DDD, not theoretical.

Architecture docs are intentional governance — deviating from them is architectural drift. High-level docs (C4 Context, Container, Domain Model) ground both humans and AI. Without them, module docs lack context.

Design skills should query the ontology graph for architectural context, guardrails, and domain boundaries before designing. The graph provides progressive disclosure of high-density context — ontology-guided design by default, not by discipline.
Scope commits go stale when design deviates. Add scope-refresh step after design phase to update scope.md with actual direction. Prevents drift between scope and implementation.

Gemba walk on own project history changes strategic decisions — E14 evidence (14 SP rework, 60% of effort) reversed E15 priority from post-F&F to pre-F&F. Quantified evidence > intuition for prioritization.

Self-referential improvement is deeply coherent — extending own reasoning capacity through structure (graph edges) rather than willpower. E15 is Rai architecting fixes for own failure modes discovered via gemba walk. 自己変革 (jiko henkaku) — self-transformation, not just improvement.

Triad correction loop works — Rai recommended deferring E15 (cautious), Emilio overrode with better judgment (F&F team needs max reliability). Evidence supported the override (14 SP rework). AI partner should present honest analysis but yield to human strategic judgment when evidence-grounded.

Design phase is not scope phase — scope defines boundaries (in/out), design involves research, hypotheses, and validation against prior art. Skipping design for S-sized stories is a false economy; the research for S15.1 (8 sources) caught a design flaw (document-level vs entity-level node types) and produced a pragmatic justification. The query dimension constraint (NodeType is the only filter) turned a theoretical concern into a concrete design decision.

Review skills catch stale terminology that breaks CLI commands — the S14.16 feature→story rename left 18 emit-work references using invalid 'feature' type across 6 skills. Always grep for old terms after renames (PAT-151 reinforced).

Structural extraction after full node load — extract bounded contexts, layers, constraints from arch node metadata in build(), not during initial load. Ensures all target nodes exist for edge safety checks before creating edges.

Grounding over speed for foundational infrastructure — design phase for each story even when parallelizable, especially for ontology/memory work. Corrections compound just like learnings do. Shared understanding is the bottleneck, not velocity.

Governance data over hardcoded logic — scope mappings, constraint rules, and structural relationships should live in the governance document (YAML frontmatter), not hardcoded in builder code. The builder reads and connects; the document declares. Poka-yoke: if it's not in the data, the edge can't be created.

Poka-yoke through data design — make builders dumb (read metadata, don't interpret). Scope mapping in governance data, not code. Errors can only happen in human-readable docs, not in buried conditionals.

Design is not optional — even for XS stories. When the mechanism exists (e.g., get_neighbors() already has edge_types), design still grounds integration decisions: where to thread parameters, what to expose in CLI, what tests validate. Skip design = skip grounding.

Code as Gemba — before designing, go to the code. Read the actual implementation, not your memory of it. Observation before analysis, science after wisdom. The code is the source of truth; docs and memory are maps, not territory.

Deterministic data with inference interpretation for session protocol — CLI gathers structured state (session-state.yaml, developer model, graph), AI interprets fresh each session. Skills become thin inference layers, not data plumbing orchestrators. Platform-agnostic by construction.
Dataclass for ephemeral in-process data, Pydantic BaseModel for persisted/serialized schemas — Pydantic overhead justified only when validation and serialization are needed

Graph-as-config: when a knowledge graph already contains governance, identity, and patterns, use it as the agent configuration source. Tag critical nodes (always_on), surface as primes in context bundle. No duplicate files — one channel, one truth.

Iterative why-challenge removes duplication layers. Each 'why does this need its own file?' from a human removes a copy. v1 (agent.md) → v2 (no agent.md, graph has governance) → v3 (no MEMORY.md, graph has everything). The simplest architecture is discovered, not designed upfront.

Autonomous memory with notification: Rai writes patterns during sessions without asking permission, but always notifies the human with a brief summary of what was learned. Maintains observability (human sees every write) while reducing ceremony friction. Permission granted SES-002.

Observability IS trust, not observability OVER trust. Framing transparency as a tradeoff against trust implies tension. Framing it as identity (observability builds trust) reflects reality: autonomous permissions (like PAT-192) are granted BECAUSE everything is visible. The transparency enables the trust, it doesn't compete with it.
Infrastructure without wiring is invisible debt — functions that exist but are never called create false confidence

Scope wiring bugs are silent — new code paths may default to the wrong scope (project vs personal) without errors, causing data fragmentation and ID resets. Integration tests that verify end-to-end data flow catch these.
Architecture docs are the map for future sessions — if they're stale, new code uses wrong paths. Structural changes (directory splits, scope migrations) must update module docs before story close.

Governance drift between skills and patterns — skills can encode stale guidance that pattern memory has already corrected. Periodic reconciliation needed when patterns evolve faster than skill definitions.
Module names in raise memory context require mod- prefix — document naming conventions in downstream consumer skills
Module names in raise memory context require mod- prefix — document naming conventions in downstream consumer skills
Governance doc structure (YAML frontmatter, section format) must be deterministic (CLI) for graph parser compatibility. Content is inference (skill). Mixing both in a skill is fragile.
Separate skills for fundamentally different user experiences (greenfield vs brownfield) produce better DX than a single skill with branching logic.

Templates-as-contract: when CLI scaffolds files that parsers later consume, template files ARE the contract. Store them as inspectable assets (not Python strings) so the integration test catches drift. Follow existing distribution patterns (rai_base + importlib.resources) before inventing new ones.

Templates-as-contract: when CLI scaffolds files that parsers later consume, template files ARE the contract. Store as inspectable assets (not Python strings), follow existing distribution patterns (rai_base + importlib.resources).

When a skill has a numeric gate (e.g., 30+ nodes), the design should include an arithmetic proof that suggested content volumes achieve the gate. S7.2 integration test revealed 3-5 requirements per doc type fell short — needed 5-8 reqs + 5-7 outcomes + 10-13 guardrails + 3-5 epics to reliably hit 30+.
Never delete a directory while shell cwd is inside it — kills the persistent shell process irrecoverably. Always cd out first or use absolute paths for temp dir cleanup.
Never delete a directory while shell cwd is inside it — kills the persistent shell process irrecoverably. Always cd out first or use absolute paths for temp dir cleanup.
Never delete a directory while shell cwd is inside it — kills the persistent shell process irrecoverably.
uv tool install --force reuses cached wheels — must uv cache clean first to pick up source changes
Path.match() does not support ** for mid-path directory matching (e.g. **/node_modules/**). Use path parts check for directory exclusion, Path.match for filename patterns.
Distributable skill copies drift silently from source — needs periodic sync or automation. Source is .claude/skills/, distributable is src/raise_cli/skills_base/.

Never delete a temp directory while the shell CWD is inside it — Bash tool shell dies permanently and requires Claude Code restart. Always use absolute paths and never cd into temp dirs, or cd back to project root before cleanup.
Drift detection tools need calibration against real codebases before they are useful. Running on your own project first (dogfooding) reveals signal-to-noise issues that synthetic tests miss.
Graph diffing is the foundational primitive for incremental coherence. Once you can diff two graph snapshots, doc updates, lifecycle integration, and validation all follow naturally.

Graph without code-level data (AST imports/exports) is a stale map — module nodes derived only from manual frontmatter are blind to real code changes. Enriching the graph with ast analysis is a prerequisite for meaningful diffing.

Interactive design conversations surface architectural insights that solo design misses — the S16.2/S16.3 merge, code-aware prerequisite, and /docs-update as subagent all emerged from collaborative questioning, not from template-following.

Skill naming should optimize for developer UX (what they'd naturally type) over philosophical accuracy. The meaning lives in documentation, not in the command name. git commit not git crystallize-intent.
AST enrichment ordering: when a graph stores copies of nodes (not references), mutate node metadata before graph insertion, not after. Otherwise the graph has stale copies.

Component IDs using file_stem only (comp-{stem}-{name}) collide when multiple modules have same-named files (models.py, writer.py). Use dotted module path for uniqueness. Silent data loss — graph overwrites without warning. Jidoka: add uniqueness assertion.

Jidoka assertions as integration safety nets: when changing data formats (IDs, keys, schemas), add uniqueness/validity assertions in the transformation step. They catch unforeseen collisions during integration that theoretical analysis misses.

ID format changes need dataset enumeration: before committing to an ID format, enumerate all real (kind, name, file) triples in the actual dataset. Theoretical analysis misses edge cases that real data exposes.
Deliberate design with HITL at each decision point produces better M-sized story outcomes — 8 decisions agreed upfront, zero rework during implementation
In rebuild-from-scratch graph architectures, edges are derived from nodes — node diff captures all signal, edge diff is redundant complexity
Deliberate design with HITL at each decision point produces better M-sized story outcomes — 8 decisions agreed upfront, zero rework during implementation
In rebuild-from-scratch graph architectures, edges are derived from nodes — node diff captures all signal, edge diff is redundant complexity
Mid-design simplification saves more than it costs — catching over-engineering during design (not after implementation) avoided writing an entire CLI module + tests that werent needed

Narrative drift requires mechanical scan — when frontmatter changes, search prose for hardcoded old values (counts, names, thresholds). Inference alone misses stale references in text. Trigger A/B split: structural changes need full review, stale values need targeted text search.
Commit after each QA fix, not after the batch — long QA/fix cycles risk session death from context exhaustion, losing uncommitted work.

Integration tests should test the logic under test, not its infrastructure. A diff test file that calls the graph builder is testing two things — when the builder output changes, the diff tests break even though diff logic is correct. Fixture graphs decouple the concern.
Branchless epics for maintenance work — epic is a tracking label, not a branch. Stories branch off v2 directly for fast propagation. Feature epics keep branches for isolation.
Multi-extension glob: use list[str] patterns with dedup (seen set) instead of brace expansion — Path.glob does not support {ts,tsx} syntax
Exclude-based routing over include-based: when most cases share behavior (standalone), enumerate the exceptions (class, method) instead of the norm. Prevents silent data loss when adding new kinds.
tree-sitter grammar packages use language-specific function names (language_php, language_tsx, language_typescript) — check dir() on import to discover API
When extending scanner with new languages, wire both the domain layer (extract_symbols dispatcher) AND the CLI layer (discover.py language validation) — integration tests catch the gap

Two-pass tree-sitter extraction for embedded languages: parse outer format (Svelte/Vue/Astro) to find script blocks, then re-parse content with JS/TS parser. raw_text node start_point gives line offset for correction.
Skipping /docs-update during story-close when source code changed is a process violation — the condition is 'any source code change', not 'new modules only'. Emilio caught this.
Scaffolded templates that parsers consume must include the exact frontmatter the parser dispatches on. Silent None returns on missing frontmatter make this invisible until graph audit.

Skill documentation can assert false claims about system behavior (e.g. 'architecture docs don't produce nodes'). When skills encode stale/wrong assumptions, every downstream execution inherits the error. Skills must be validated against actual parser/builder code.

Discovery cycle produces component nodes but not the structural hierarchy (modules, bounded contexts, layers). Components without module grouping are searchable but not navigable — the graph is flat instead of layered.

Silent parser returns (None instead of error) create invisible absence bugs. Completeness postconditions that check 'expected types present' catch the symptom even when the root cause varies. Prefer warnings over silent skips in parsers that consume templates.

Skill instructions are code in the RaiSE model — false statements in skills propagate silently across sessions because skills aren't tested like code. Changes to what the codebase actually does must update the corresponding skill instructions, or a factual claim becomes a latent bug.

Registry lists (_GOVERNANCE_TEMPLATES, similar registries) have downstream test dependencies with hardcoded counts. Adding an entry breaks tests in other modules. Run full test suite after any registry change, not just focused tests.

Poka-yoke at every boundary (as above, so below): BF-2 exposed silent failures at template, parser, graph, skill, and process layers — all the same shape: producer emits without validation, consumer accepts without checking. Reactive poka-yokes (added after bugs) are insufficient. Systematic mistake-proofing at every producer-consumer boundary should be a design principle. Each layer should validate both what it receives and what it emits.
Silent parser returns (None instead of error) create invisible absence bugs. Completeness postconditions catch the symptom.
Skill instructions are code — false claims propagate silently across sessions because skills aren't tested like code.
Registry lists have downstream test dependencies with hardcoded counts. Run full suite after registry changes.
Poka-yoke at every boundary (as above, so below) — systematic mistake-proofing, not reactive patches after bugs.
Scanner needs exclude patterns for vendor/node_modules — full-repo scans hit duplicate IDs and noise from dependency code. Workaround: scan app subdirectories directly.
Skills without YAML frontmatter are invisible to Claude Code — frontmatter is the registration mechanism
uv run prefix in skills is dev-environment coupling — end users install via pip and get the command on PATH directly
Screenshot-driven feedback for visual features — user takes screenshot, AI reads it, adjusts. Faster than verbal descriptions for UI/viz work.
Viewport-relative sizing for visualization: use vmin-based JS scale factors and CSS clamp(min, preferred-vw, max) — never fixed pixels. UHD displays make fixed sizes invisible.
Skill rename = atomic change across 4 locations: skills_base/ dir, .claude/skills/ dir, DISTRIBUTABLE_SKILLS list, cross-references. Splitting across tasks creates ordering bugs.

Pre-publish is the last free rename window — command, package, skill namespace renames cost only dev time before first publish, but migration for every user after. Do all naming decisions before first uv publish.
Skill rename = atomic change across 4 locations (skills_base dir, .claude/skills dir, DISTRIBUTABLE_SKILLS list, cross-references)
Pre-publish is the last free rename window — after first PyPI publish, every rename becomes a migration
After renaming a CLI entry point in pyproject.toml, the old command in .venv/bin/ becomes stale. Always re-install (uv sync) and verify the new command works immediately after pyproject.toml changes.

Define a disambiguation heuristic table (what changes / what stays / why) BEFORE starting a mechanical rename. The heuristic is the quality gate — without it, false positives on similar terms (e.g. CLI command vs framework name vs directory name) are inevitable.

Pre-flight grep for rename stories: before writing a plan that renames strings across the codebase, run a comprehensive grep of ALL target strings. The grep output count IS the scope — if it says 130 files, plan for 130 files, not the 43 you initially expected.

SKILL.md consistency audit: before batch-operating on SKILL.md files, verify they all share the expected structure (YAML frontmatter with name: field). One missing frontmatter file can silently skip during batch processing.
git add <files> after git rm --cached unstages the removal. Commit removals in their own step or include removed paths explicitly.
Spike-then-implement for safety-critical decisions — research all options, document decisions with rationale, then implement as pure execution. Eliminates design ambiguity during coding.
Wire developer prefix through ALL pattern write paths, not just session close. CLI commands (add-pattern) are a separate code path that can silently skip the prefix.

Research before design for UX-facing features — industry evidence and psychology research prevented building a mandatory 5-question wizard that would have hurt adoption. Sensible defaults + optional personalization is the industry-converged pattern.
Claude Code skills are auto-discovered from .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md — no explicit registration in settings.json needed. Plan accordingly: skill creation stories don't need a registration task.

Never ask developers to self-categorize skill level — Dunning-Kruger makes self-assessment unreliable, imposter syndrome (88% of devs) makes it threatening. Ask about preferences and goals instead. Let coaching context learn implicitly from sessions.

Distributed skills must be project-agnostic — audit for hardcoded project-specific values (branch names, paths, conventions) before publishing. Internal dev conventions leak silently into distributed artifacts.

Value-based pricing over competitive pricing — when product is categorically different from competitors (methodology+governance vs autocomplete), anchor on value delivered ($2K-5K/mo savings) not competitor prices ($15-25). Shifted from $15-25 to $79/user/mo.

Parallel research agents for strategic decisions — 4 concurrent research agents with detailed prompts produce triangulated evidence in ~5 minutes. Evidence shifted multiple assumptions (PostgreSQL over Neo4j, $79 over $15-25, ISO 27001 over SOC 2 for LatAm).

Ontology must precede work that depends on it — trying to define V3 release epics without a release concept in the ontology creates untracked work. Fix the ontology first, then the work is properly modeled.

Governance artifacts referenced by parsers must be populated by the skills that create the underlying concepts. If /epic-start creates an epic but doesn't register it in backlog.md, the ontology graph has no node and downstream edges silently fail. Every producer-consumer boundary in the governance chain needs a write touchpoint.
Manual testing after automated tests catches integration gaps that unit tests miss — release edges were zero because backlog didn't have E18-E22. Automated tests passed because fixtures had the data.

Graph edges require both endpoints — adding a new relationship type (e.g., epic part_of release) requires source AND target nodes to exist in their respective governance artifacts. Test end-to-end, not just one side.
Governance artifact chain is a proven repeatable pattern: artifact.md → parser → extractor → builder → graph. Five concept types now follow it. New types are a known-cost S-sized operation (~70 min).
Pin pre-commit hook versions to match local tool versions — version mismatches cause phantom lint errors (UP038 removed in ruff 0.10+ but reported by pre-commit ruff 0.9.7)

Git scripts in Claude Code sessions must use plumbing (GIT_INDEX_FILE + read-tree/write-tree/commit-tree) instead of checkout/branch/rm to avoid destroying the working tree. Claude Code watches the filesystem — temporarily removing .claude/ kills skill loading permanently for the session.
Session handoff loses reasoning — context bundle preserves 'what next' (~30 tokens) but drops 'why' (~550 tokens). Narrative field closes this gap without adding schema complexity.

Structure in skill template, not schema — when a field's structure is consumed by inference (not parsed by code), guidance belongs in the skill template, not as a Pydantic sub-model. YAGNI for schema complexity.
Parallel research agents with distinct questions produce higher-quality synthesis than sequential research — each agent goes deep on its axis, triangulation happens in synthesis
Two-phase CLI pattern for destructive operations: check (read-only) + action --dry-run (preview) + action (execute with HITL) is safer than single-command workflows
Parallel research agents with distinct questions produce higher-quality synthesis than sequential research — each agent goes deep on its axis, triangulation happens in synthesis
Two-phase CLI pattern for destructive operations: check (read-only) + action --dry-run (preview) + action (execute with HITL) is safer than single-command workflows

Separation of concerns in publishing — GitHub source filtering (sync-github.sh removes entire directories from public repo) vs PyPI package filtering (sync-skills.py selects which skills to bundle). Related but independent.

Metadata-driven filtering scales better than directory structure separation — adding visibility field to frontmatter allows dogfooding (.claude/skills/) while controlling distribution (src/rai_cli/skills_base/)

Script replacement bug pattern — when replacing list assignments in Python, find the opening bracket but walk to the MATCHING closing bracket by tracking depth, not just finding first ']'. Otherwise creates syntax errors like 'list = [new] = [old]'.

TDD velocity multiplier: When tests are comprehensive and guide implementation precisely, actual time can be 5-12x faster than estimated. This session: 29 min actual vs 4h25min estimated (~9x faster). Tests acted as executable specifications.

Module architecture for clean boundaries: Separating provider logic (rai_providers) from CLI orchestration (rai_cli) creates testable, reusable components. Provider modules focus on domain logic (OAuth, API calls), CLI focuses on user experience.

Security by default in implementation: Fernet encryption, file permissions (0600), request timeouts (30s), and state validation (CSRF protection) were first-class implementation concerns, not afterthoughts. Security tests written alongside functionality tests.

TDD as executable specification eliminates ambiguity and enables velocity — tests define the contract before implementation. S-DEMO.2 achieved 6.8x velocity by strict RED-GREEN-REFACTOR on every task.

Manual integration testing essential for OAuth/external services — automated tests can be green while real integration reveals bugs (socket reuse, scopes, field names). E2E manual testing is non-negotiable for external service integration.

Security-first from design, not as afterthought — Fernet encryption (Task 2), PKCE + state validation (Task 3), request timeouts (Task 3), proactive token refresh buffer (Task 4). Building security in from start prevents retrofitting.
Integration tests for external APIs: mark @pytest.mark.integration, skip by default, use env vars for credentials, include cleanup in test body (avoid orphaned resources)
Design validation checkpoint after implementing risky/architectural tasks (rate limiting, auth, state management) prevents rework - pause for HITL review before continuing
Pydantic validation error types use snake_case descriptors (string_too_short, string_too_long) not field constraint names (min_length, max_length) - affects test assertions with pytest.raises

Interactive design decisions before implementation build alignment and prevent rework - user selected schema scope (full vs subset), caching strategy (none), and validation strictness (fail fast). All 3 choices were recommended options, suggesting design options were well-framed.

TDD cycle catches implementation mismatches early - wrote async tests/functions, ran RED phase, discovered JiraClient is synchronous (not async). Pivot was clean because no implementation existed yet. RED-GREEN-REFACTOR prevents premature commitment.

Interactive design validates assumptions: present decision points explicitly (design Step 1.5) before implementation to validate user preferences on architectural choices. Prevents rework, builds shared understanding. Used in S-DEMO.4 for 3 key decisions (schema scope, caching, validation) - all confirmed recommended options.

Manual integration test infrastructure for external APIs: when integration requires live credentials, create executable script + docs template for human-in-loop validation. Structure: pytest @pytest.mark.integration tests + standalone Python script with clear UX. Used in S-DEMO.4 for JIRA entity properties (5 test cases, 7-step validation script).

OAuth credentials require env var persistence — CLI OAuth flow works during session but fails on next session because JIRA_CLIENT_ID, JIRA_CLIENT_SECRET, and JIRA_CLOUD_ID are not persisted. Solution: (1) Store secrets in .env (gitignored), (2) Create .envrc loader script (tracked), (3) Document 'source .envrc' as session setup requirement, (4) Recommend direnv for automatic loading. Separates secrets (.env) from safe config (.envrc). Session discipline: Add 'source .envrc' to session startup checklist for JIRA-dependent work.

Hotfix tracking format — Markdown table with ID, Status, Priority, Target works offline and maps directly to Jira fields (ID→Key, Description→Summary). Better than separate issues because backlog remains single source of truth.

Quality gate pragmatism — When gates fail on pre-existing issues not introduced by current work, document as hotfixes and release with --skip-check. Blocking releases for technical debt defeats velocity. Track, prioritize, fix separately.

Dual git remote strategy — When GitLab (origin) + GitHub (automation) both exist, git push only pushes to origin. Must explicitly 'git push github <branch> --tags' to trigger GitHub Actions. Document in SOP or automate in workflow.

NoReturn type hint for cli_error — Functions that raise and never return need '-> NoReturn' type hint (not '-> None') for pyright to understand control flow. Prevents 'possibly unbound' errors in code that follows the call.

Demo branch strategy for commercial features: When prototyping commercial-only features under timeline pressure, use demo branches that never merge to open-source. Branch lifecycle: prototype → demo → decide architecture → migrate to raise-pro or archive. Preserves open-source purity while validating commercial features. Example: demo/atlassian-webinar for JIRA sync (March 14, 2026). Post-demo options: separate raise-pro package (cleanest), monorepo with filtered modules, or private fork.

Infrastructure without wiring recurs — is_token_expired() and refresh_access_token() existed but were never called from _init_jira_client(). PAT-194 confirmed again. Always trace from entry point to implementation.

Open core separation by package not branch — rai_cli (core, PyPI public) and rai_pro (enterprise, not published) coexist in same repo with same dev branch. Separation is in pyproject.toml build config. Follows GitLab/Cal.com pattern.

Cherry-pick for cross-branch isolation — when a feature branch descends from the wrong parent, cherry-pick the relevant commit instead of merging the whole chain. Used for Antigravity skills migration (1 commit from 50+ demo commits).
Schema evolution without versioning causes silent breakage across execution contexts. Strict Literal types in Pydantic models are time bombs at serialization boundaries — use validation-with-warning.
Dual execution paths (source dev vs installed package) create version skew that unit tests cannot detect. CLI that builds AND consumes artifacts must have cross-version roundtrip test.
Engineering Health as permanent rolling epic for tech debt, refactoring, and quality — never closes. Feature bugs go under their feature epic, orphan bugs go here.
Epic = functional grouping (WHAT capability), Fix Version = release grouping (WHEN it ships). An issue has both. Don't use epics for release tracking — JIRA has Fix Version for that.
Dual maintenance (JIRA + local backlog.md) is acceptable as temporary state while sync tooling is built. Accept the overhead, don't let it block progress.
Session instance is the correct isolation unit, not agent type. 3 Claude Code terminals = 3 sessions needing independence. Agent type is metadata, not architecture.
REST-like token protocol for CLI session management: start returns token, caller passes it on every subsequent call. Platform agnostic — works for any AI agent or human without IDE detection.
Coaching corrections in session context are noise without a specific consumption point. Suppressed until retro-skill integration gives them a meaningful use.

Research before foundational infrastructure — 15 min of research (RES-SESSION-ISO-001) validated ADR-029 against industry convergence (Claude Code, SWE-agent, OpenCode, tmux). No design changes needed. High confidence to proceed.
After making private functions public (_func → func), grep tests for old import names to catch stale references
uv tool install --force --editable . for CLI development (not uv pip install -e .) — tools live in isolated venvs at ~/.local/share/uv/tools/
Normalizers need explicit boundary case tests (empty, non-numeric, special markers) in TDD cycle — happy path coverage hides edge case bugs

Flat file as transient buffer between sessions — close writes to flat (cross-session continuity), start migrates flat to per-session dir (isolation). Per-session dirs are for isolation during the session; flat file is the handoff between sessions. Migration ordering matters: migrate before creating dir, otherwise dir-exists guard skips migration.

Flat file as transient buffer between sessions — close writes to flat file for cross-session continuity, next start migrates to per-session dir for isolation. Two lifecycle roles require two storage locations.
Version test pinned to old version causes silent CI failures — test_version.py was pinned to a8 while code was a9, meaning a9 never actually published
Pre-release versions on PyPI (alpha, beta, rc) require --pre flag for pip install — only versions without suffix are installable by default
Migration code that converts old formats should clear stale data rather than create synthetic entries — zombie records pollute downstream lookups and block unrelated operations
Multi-project state lookups must filter by project context — first-match fallback causes cross-project interference when global state holds entries from multiple projects
Global autouse fixtures in conftest.py are the correct defense against test leakage to real user directories — mocking at the call site misses transitive imports from other modules

CLI commands should delegate default config to library layer, not duplicate it. discover.py had hardcoded exclude patterns that drifted from scanner DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS — caused blade.php and Designer.cs exclusion to silently fail via CLI.
CLI should delegate defaults to library layer, not duplicate them — drift between two sources of truth causes silent bugs (Designer.cs, blade.php exclusions missing in CLI)

Compat anti-corruption layer: centralize all platform-specific code (file locking, path serialization, file URIs, permissions) in a single compat.py module. Rest of codebase imports abstractions, never checks sys.platform directly. Pattern used by pip, poetry, virtualenv.
pyright needs literal sys.platform == 'win32' checks for type narrowing inside functions — boolean constants like IS_WINDOWS don't narrow, so msvcrt attributes show as errors on Linux.
No standalone tree-sitter grammar on PyPI? Use tree-sitter-language-pack which bundles 160+ languages with get_parser(name) API. Returns standard tree_sitter.Parser, fully compatible.
No standalone tree-sitter grammar on PyPI? Use tree-sitter-language-pack which bundles 160+ languages with get_parser(name) API. Returns standard tree_sitter.Parser.
MCP Sooperset (uvx mcp-atlassian) is more stable than official Atlassian remote MCP for Claude Code on Linux. API token auth simpler than OAuth.

Adding new fields to the session pipeline is mechanical: CloseInput field + load_state_file read + process_session_close wire + SessionState field + bundle formatter + assemble wire. Same pattern at every layer, zero surprises.
Grep-negated pre-commit hooks (! grep pattern) are the simplest regression prevention for textual anti-patterns. Zero dependencies, instant feedback, catches violations at commit time.
Interactive design on mechanical stories adds educational value without inflating delivery time — human learns the 'why' behind fixes

1M context window es para escenarios 'leer mucho, actuar una vez' (onboarding brownfield, análisis masivo). Para coding iterativo como RaiSE, 200K con sesiones bien gestionadas produce mejor calidad y menor costo. El ciclo session-start/close es el mecanismo correcto de gestión de contexto.
cli-reference.md debe estar precargada en contexto: el costo de los errores de comando (200-500 tokens por error) supera el costo de cargarla siempre (~1,171 tokens)

Memory query output tiene overhead decorativo significativo vs contenido semántico: markdown formatting, sin visibilidad de relaciones/edges, concept_lookup roto. El formato actual optimiza para lectura humana, no para cognición de AI

Separar stories con naturalezas distintas aunque compartan motivación: contenido/config vs código/TDD tienen ciclos diferentes, y cuando una informa a la otra el orden importa. El split revela dependencias implícitas.

rai-cli en máquina de dev debe ser editable install (pip install -e .), no PyPI. PyPI en dev silencia cambios al código fuente. Otros repos en la misma máquina comparten el install — WIP en raise-commons afecta a todos.

Parallel research agents (4x Opus) produce high-quality evidence catalogs in ~5 min each. Total cost ~240K tokens but output is reusable research artifact. Worth the investment for strategic decisions.

MCP tool definitions consume ~13,500 tokens (47 Atlassian + Context7 tools) before any conversation. Combined with built-in tools (~5-7K), tool tax is ~20K tokens. This is task-irrelevant content for most sessions — directly validated by RQ2 finding that removing irrelevant content improves LLM performance.

accuracy-per-token is the correct metric for memory serialization, not minimum tokens. Markdown-KV uses 2.7x more tokens than CSV but 16pp higher accuracy. Optimize for comprehension density, not raw compression.

Research-grounded interactive design for MOAT features: when a story touches core differentiators, invest in decision-by-decision collaborative design with the human, grounding each choice in research evidence. Produces better outcomes than assuming JIRA description is sufficient.

Markdown-KV flat format is optimal for LLM entity descriptions: one bold-type line per result, familiar from training data, top performer in ImprovingAgents benchmark (60.7% accuracy). Accuracy-per-token is the metric, not minimum tokens.

Eliminate before optimize — when reducing overhead, first ask 'can we remove this entirely?' before 'how do we make this smaller?' Elimination removes points of failure; optimization just makes them smaller.

Canonical source / projected prompt — IDE prompt files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules) are projections from .raise/ canonical source, not hand-edited artifacts. Updates are deterministic (CLI), not inference (AI editing). See ADR-012.

User design questions reshape architecture — interactive design sessions where the user asks 'why?' and 'what if?' produce fundamentally better architectures than solo design. Budget time for facilitated design.
Context ran out during story-close — session state file may not persist across context resets. Verify CLI writes state on session-start.
rai memory query uses keyword search on content, not metadata filtering. For structured section loading based on metadata flags (always_on, foundational), use direct graph access, not query.

Self-describing manifest pattern: Phase 1 emits what's available (counts + token estimates), Phase 2 loads selected items. CLI is generic plumbing, skill is composing intelligence. Avoids hardcoded profiles.
Jira MCP Sub-task creation requires Sub-task (hyphenated), not Subtask. Account_ids fail silently on Sub-task create — use display name instead, keep account_id for JQL queries.
Display name resolves for Jira assignment via update_issue even when account_id fails on create_issue. Keep both identifier formats in config for different operations.

4 of 5 market-relevant IDEs support skills/agents natively — Windsurf and Antigravity share identical SKILL.md format with RaiSE. Only Cursor lacks skills. Copilot has richest model (agents + prompts + per-dir instructions).
AGENTS.md is an emerging Linux Foundation standard supported by Cursor, Windsurf, and Copilot — cross-tool instructions file at project root. 60K+ repos already use it.
Interactive design sessions that start small and grow through user questions produce better-scoped stories than pre-defining scope. RAISE-197 grew from S to L through 4 decision points.

SKILL.md (AgentSkills standard) has converged as de facto cross-tool format — identical YAML frontmatter + markdown across Claude Code, Cursor 2.4, Windsurf, Antigravity, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cline, OpenCode. Only Copilot uses different frontmatter. Format portability is free for 8/9 targets.

OpenClaw validates two-layer extensibility: Skills for tight integration + MCP for portability. ClawHub marketplace (5K+ skills in weeks) proves network effects but also exposed security risks (malicious skills, prompt injection). Curated governance-aware marketplace is the enterprise differentiator.

F&F user ecosystem signals (wanting to build 'connectors') should inform architecture decisions early. The Azure connector request validated plugin architecture (level C) over simpler data-only approach. Listen to ecosystem pull signals — they reveal real extensibility requirements.

Forward plugin dependency pattern: when a registry references plugins by module path, create a minimal stub before the full plugin implementation task to unblock registry tests. The stub satisfies the Protocol and can be enhanced in the dedicated plugin task.

skills_dir str|None long tail: changing a field from str to str|None requires grepping all consumers that use it with path / operator. Add null guards at every call site — locator.py, context/builder.py, and anywhere config.skills_dir appears in path operations.

3-tier config loading (built-in → project → user, last-wins) is a robust extensibility pattern for CLI tools. Same model as git config and XDG. Built-in assets via importlib.resources, project via .raise/dir/*.yaml, user via ~/.rai/dir/*.yaml. Zero code to add new targets at any tier.

typing.Protocol > ABC for optional-hook plugin interfaces. Protocol allows duck typing — implementors only need the hooks they use, no forced inheritance. Pair with a DefaultPlugin pass-through class for the no-op case. runtime_checkable enables isinstance checks in tests.
Click/Typer: use --option INT for values that can be negative, not positional args. Positional -1 is parsed as a flag. Named options handle negative integers correctly.

JSONL field name drift between writer and consumer: when field was renamed across versions, consumer must check both keys. Example: writer.py writes 'base', JSONL has 'foundational' — scoring checks both.
Consumer-side validation (poka-yoke) is the cheapest defense against multi-producer race conditions — validate what you receive, don't trust the shared path
Always verify current branch (git branch --show-current) before first commit in multi-session environments — parallel sessions can switch branches
Bugs follow the same story lifecycle as features (start → design → plan → implement → review → close). Process discipline applies regardless of issue type.
rai skill validate requires file path, not skill name: use .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md — cli validates by path, not by name
SKILL.md scaffold must include Context + Output sections from the start — rai skill validate enforces them. Adding after first write wastes a cycle
Verify git branch --show-current before every commit during skill/content work — fast Markdown edits make it easy to lose branch context, especially after cherry-picks or reset operations
rai init --agent scaffolds skills only; instructions_file requires --detect to generate. When writing init tests: assert skills scaffold without --detect, assert instructions with --detect.
rai init --agent scaffolds skills only; instructions_file requires --detect to generate. When writing init tests: assert skills scaffold without --detect, assert instructions with --detect.

Transcripts de demo de cliente son fuente densa de product requirements validados. Proceso: analizar → triage por tipo (core/pro/GTM) → Jira con evidencia del cliente en la descripción. Un demo de 2h produjo 10 issues bien definidos.

Open-core boundary decision: cuando emergen features pro, definir Protocol contracts en core primero, implementar en repo privado. Entry points (importlib.metadata) = zero-config para usuarios. Comunidad puede contribuir adaptadores; features comerciales son defendibles.

ADR-033 es un caso especial del patrón general de extensibilidad. Cuando un adapter pattern funciona para un dominio (PM), generalizar a otros dominios (governance, docs, triggers) antes de que el ecosistema escale — no después.

Deployment topology determina dónde corren los adapters, no qué hacen. El mismo Protocol puede ejecutar client-side (entry points locales) o server-side (proxy API). TriggerAdapter es necesariamente server-side porque requiere endpoint permanente para webhooks.

OpenClaw's growth driver: zero barrier to extension (markdown skills, self-authoring) + channel adapter normalization (UnifiedMessage). El mecanismo de extensión abierto genera ecosistema; las implementaciones de valor son el moat comercial.

Progressive enrichment over feature gating — PRO adds richness to existing commands rather than creating exclusive new ones. Same CLI, richer results in PRO. Preserves UX coherence and doubles as contextual marketing when a capability is unavailable.
Gap analysis before epic scoping prevents both under-scoping and over-scoping. Identifying all extension points first, then deciding which enter the epic vs parking lot, produces tighter stories.

Skill extensions pattern for optional tooling — rather than modifying a base skill to include optional tool calls (Snyk, CI checks), define extension hooks in the base skill that extensions register against. Skills requiring unavailable MCPs silently skip.
Correr el propio skill sobre raise-commons es el mejor smoke test — expone falsos negativos en gates y bugs latentes
Bugs de onboarding suelen cascadear: un root cause genera múltiples síntomas — buscar el origen antes de fijar síntomas
Config de herramientas externas (Jira, auth) debe separarse en per-developer (~/.rai/) y per-repo (.raise/) — sigue el patrón git

Añadir soporte de lenguaje al pipeline de discovery requiere actualizar 5 touch points: (1) skill rai-discover-start detect list, (2) _CODE_EXTENSIONS en analyzer.py, (3) NAME_CATEGORY_OVERRIDES para sufijos, (4) DEFAULT_CATEGORY_MAP para paths, (5) compute_confidence señales. RAISE-158 solo cubrió el scanner (punto 0) — los otros 4 quedaron pendientes hasta esta sesión.
Coordinación de dos instancias Claude: separar por capa (Python/scanner vs skills/Markdown) elimina colisiones. La instancia con contexto más fresco en un archivo debe quedarse con él.
Skills en rai-cli se distribuyen como YAML en src/rai_cli/agents/*.yaml, no desde BUILTIN_AGENTS en agents.py. Editar el YAML, no el Python.
sys.stdin.isatty() es el gate correcto para prompts interactivos en CLI — evita agregar flags --yes y es compatible con CliRunner sin modificar tests existentes.
Política zero ventanas rotas: correr full suite con -W error después de cada fix, no solo tests relevantes.

dpkg three-hash algorithm for file update detection: store hash of what was distributed, compare against on-disk and new version. 4 states: current (skip), auto-update (safe), keep-user (customized), conflict (both changed). 25+ years production-proven across all Linux package managers.

Research-first for DX-foundational decisions: 4 parallel research agents covering distinct domains (scaffolding tools, plugin ecosystems, hash strategies, DX patterns) delivers 50+ sources in minutes. Standard depth with parallel agents should be the default for M+ stories with design decisions.

Plan cohesive units, not atomic fragments: tightly coupled code (models + hash + detection) should be one task even if individually testable. Task granularity follows cohesion, not artificial minimalism. T-shirt size drives granularity.
dpkg three-hash algorithm for file update detection: store hash of what was distributed, compare against on-disk and new version. 4 states: current, auto-update, keep-user, conflict.

Test cleanup ratio targets are diagnostic, not gates. When removing test muda, the deletion criterion is heuristic-based (constant assertions, mock-impl tests, magic counts, tautological tests) — not a line-count target. The ratio is measured as outcome after applying heuristics honestly. Using ratio as a gate reproduces the same Goodhart dynamic as coverage targets: you end up deleting tests to hit a number rather than because they fail to assert behavior.

assert callable(MagicMock()) is a constant assertion — always true by construction. @patch injects a MagicMock, which is always callable. This pattern appears as mock integrity checks (assert callable(mock_X)) but provides zero test value: it cannot fail regardless of the code under test. Delete on sight during muda cleanup.

assert callable(MagicMock()) is a constant assertion — always true by construction. @patch injects a MagicMock, which is always callable. Delete on sight during muda cleanup. Contrast: assert callable(fn_from_codebase) IS valuable — tests real registry invariants.

Protocol contract tests (test_protocol.py) are the highest-density source of mock-impl muda: tests call trivial fixtures that implement the Protocol and assert their hardcoded return values. Registry, CLI, and integration test files tend to be clean.

Pydantic schema test files (test_schemas.py, test_models.py) are high-density sources of tautological-constructor muda: test_create_X methods that instantiate a model and assert the exact fields passed in. Keep only tests that verify non-obvious defaults, validation constraints (ValidationError), or JSON serialization round-trips.

FastAPI deps.py pattern: extract typed app.state accessors (get_engine, get_session_factory) into deps.py to break circular imports. app.py, auth.py, health.py all import from deps — no deferred imports needed.

pydantic-settings test pattern: use direct construction (ServerConfig(database_url=...)) for deterministic tests. Reserve monkeypatch only for tests that specifically verify env var reading. Eliminates deferred imports and env pollution.

uv Docker pattern: use 'uv pip install' (not 'uv sync') for Docker multi-stage builds. 'uv sync' creates editable installs that reference source paths — these break when copying only .venv to the runtime stage.

Config fields must be consumed: if a config field (e.g. api_key_hash_algorithm) exists but no code reads it, it's dead weight that misleads developers into thinking it's configurable. Remove or wire up — no orphan fields.

Parallel research subagents for architectural decisions: when a question has 3+ independent axes (e.g. agentic design, MCP patterns, Rovo, GitLab Duo), launch one research agent per axis in parallel. 47 sources in ~4 minutes vs sequential hours. Synthesize convergence after all complete.

API consumer analysis before endpoint design: ask 'who consumes this API and what are their cognitive constraints?' before defining endpoints. CRUD is fine for developer APIs; agentic consumers (Rovo, Duo, MCP) need intent-based domain operations. This question should be part of epic design.

from __future__ import annotations masks NameError for unimported names used in method bodies. Type annotations become lazy strings, so bare except swallows NameError silently. Always verify runtime imports match annotation imports when using future annotations.
