Subagent worktrees need careful merge — they may rewrite shared files from scratch causing conflicts
DISTRIBUTABLE_SKILLS is a manual registry — new skills must be added or they won't deploy via sync/init
Doctor and migration are the same tool — config version detection should be built into doctor from the start
Skills vs CLI distinction in user-facing docs: humans use skills (/rai-story-start), skills orchestrate CLI. Guides must show skills, not raw CLI.
Cloudflare Pages production branch must match deploy --branch flag. Preview != Production. Always verify production URL after deploy.
Parallel worktree stories: independent stories touching different files can run simultaneously via Agent tool with isolation:worktree, then merge sequentially to dev.
Blog posts for releases should lead with user value (what can I do now?), not internal achievements (we documented things). Audience is evaluators, not team.
Module move with mock.patch: grep for patch('old.module.' targets, not just 'from old.module import'. Patches bind to module path, not class identity — shim re-exports don't redirect patches.

Infrastructure decoupling via own models: when moving infra out of a domain package, give it its own models (McpHealthResult vs AdapterHealth) to break the import dependency. Domain adapters convert at the boundary.

CLI commands are agent tools, not human interfaces. Every CLI capability that humans need should have a companion skill that provides guided, conversational interaction. The skill orchestrates the CLI — the human never constructs CLI commands directly.
Gemba data from epic design bootstraps audit stories — reduces duplication when S1 is an audit
Language-agnostic skill design: universal checks first, then language-specific sections with equivalent checks per language. Detect language from manifest or file extensions.
Configuration over convention for toolchain commands: manifest project.test_command always wins over language detection table. User insight: 'you may forget convention'.

Language detection chain: 1) manifest override, 2) manifest language field, 3) file extension scanning, 4) language table defaults, 5) ask developer. Apply consistently across all skills that run toolchain commands.
semgrep-mcp PyPI package is deprecated — use semgrep mcp built-in. uvx isolation prevents seeing system binaries; uv tool install bundles dependencies correctly.
Manifest extension pattern: add optional Pydantic model (field: Type | None = None) to ProjectManifest. Follows TierConfig, BacklogConfig precedent. Zero backward-compat risk.
QR re-run catches what first pass missed: side effects on preexisting tests from new code paths

YAML file store over markdown parsing for structured data: when operations need CRUD semantics (search, create, transition, comments, links), use YAML files validated by Pydantic models instead of regex-parsing markdown tables. Machine-first storage with CLI-first human access.

Design pivot during story is healthy when human feedback corrects approach early: S347.2 pivoted from markdown parsing to YAML store during design phase based on human input. The pivot added a design commit but eliminated all the fragile regex parsing that would have been the main source of bugs.

Legacy fallback alongside new implementation: keep old code paths behind a feature flag (_use_yaml property) while building the new path. Lets you ship incremental progress without breaking existing users. Remove legacy in a later cleanup task.
Context saturation beyond ~60% degrades LLM response quality 15-25%. Orchestrators that chain 8+ skills in single context will compress later phases.
CLAUDE.local.md is for personal project preferences only (sandbox URLs, test data). Dynamic state (epic status, branch) should come from live queries, not static files.

ThreadPoolExecutor timeout boundary: when wrapping resolve_adapter + get_issue in a single thread, the timeout covers the whole operation including adapter startup. But moving resolve_adapter inside the thread means SystemExit (from sys.exit) must be caught as BaseException — it escapes the thread and surfaces in future.result().

Graceful degradation pattern: never-fail functions that query external services should catch Exception AND BaseException subtypes (SystemExit, KeyboardInterrupt) separately, returning a degraded result with a descriptive warning rather than bubbling. This keeps the caller path clean.

In-process adapter call vs subprocess: when the adapter is importable, calling resolve_adapter() + adapter.get_issue() in-process saves ~500ms vs subprocess (rai backlog get) and preserves type safety. Use subprocess only when adapter is in a different process boundary.
Prose-only skill edits: design doc with explicit change map (file, section, old text, new text) makes each task mechanical. grep verification as TDD substitute for non-code changes.
Conditional CLI steps in skills: use 'if ticket exists' guard to keep backlog integration optional. Not all stories/epics are tracked externally — forcing transitions breaks lightweight usage.
chore(s347.3): persist pattern PAT-E-624
Label-first search strategy: use deterministic labels (rai:{work_id}) before fuzzy text search — exact match eliminates false positives from partial string matching

Adapter-aware branching via isinstance: when behavior differs by adapter type, isinstance dispatch in a single function is simpler than polymorphic method — acceptable for 2-3 branches, refactor to strategy pattern at 4+

Interactive design yields better decisions: when story has non-obvious tradeoffs (e.g., adapter-aware vs adapter-agnostic query), interactive design conversation with human produces higher-quality design than solo design phase
atomic_write via temp file + os.replace: write to .tmp suffix in same directory, then os.replace for atomic rename — avoids partial writes on adapter failure, cross-platform safe
adapter type guard before sync: detect filesystem-only adapters early with informative ValueError rather than letting them fail mid-operation — fail-fast principle applied to adapter capabilities
pipe escaping in markdown tables: backlog fields (summaries, labels) can contain pipe characters that break markdown table formatting — always escape | to \| in cell content
integration conftest.py: shared fixtures (file_adapter, backlog_dir, write_yaml_item) in conftest.py keep test module focused on assertions, not setup — extract helpers when 3+ tests share setup

yaml.safe_load over private methods: when tests need to verify persisted state, read YAML directly from disk with yaml.safe_load rather than accessing adapter internals (_load_item) — tests stay coupled to contract, not implementation

test-only stories complete faster when plan is risk-ordered: T1 protocol parity first catches adapter bugs early, subsequent tests (hook, session, sync) can rely on known-good adapter — risk-first ordering in test stories eliminates cascading failures
Documentation-only changes have deceptive blast radius — grep for stale references BEFORE committing, not after. Separate live files from historical artifacts when scoping cleanup.

For orchestrator skill constraints that MUST survive across LLM sessions (e.g., depth limits, invocation rules), use a blockquote with bold header format: > **Constraint Name — rule.** followed by rationale. This makes constraints visually prominent and grep-findable.

Documentation-only stories (SKILL.md prompt engineering) need structural verification (grep checks) instead of pytest TDD. The plan should specify verification commands upfront. This avoids the awkward 'no code, no tests' gap in the standard TDD workflow.
YAML file store over markdown for structured CRUD — deterministic round-trips
Interactive design yields better decisions for non-obvious tradeoffs
Subagent delegation per skill phase scales well for epic-sized work
Validation stories benefit from front-loading evidence into design -- design becomes the report blueprint, implementation is assembly
Weighted parity methodology template: methodology → evidence → comparison table → weighted calculation → declaration → caveats → recommendations. Reusable for any quality gate validation.
methodology.yaml branch section must use {development_branch} placeholder, not literal branch names. YAML flow items with braces need quoting.
Tests using Path('.relative') are vulnerable to CWD pollution from other tests using os.chdir(). Use Path(__file__).resolve() for CI-safe absolute paths.
CLI tests that invoke real checks against CWD must mock the check layer to isolate CLI wiring from environment state.
QR agents may not know current framework versions — verify critical findings against actual build/runtime before acting on them. Astro 5 changed output modes.
Content stories need source-of-truth verification — docs drift faster than expected. Always check actual CLI help output and skill counts against runtime.
wrangler pages deploy without --branch=main goes to preview, not production. Always use --branch=main for production deploys.
Audit framing matters — 'what would a senior dev check in 15 minutes' produces better scope than 'run security checklist'. Human evaluation criteria > tool-driven checklists.
Always verify remote URLs before bulk-replacing repo references. git remote -v is the source of truth, not docs or memory.
PyPI pending publishers are limited to one at a time. For multi-package monorepos, use an account-scoped API token for first publish, then add Trusted Publishers per-project afterward.
Scope coverage measurements to the published package (--cov=src/raise_cli), not the entire src/ tree which may include unpublished code with different coverage profiles.

Pragmatic per-file ignores for tests: ANN001/ANN201 (test args/returns don't need annotations) and D100/D103/D104 (test docstrings are noise). Per-file ignores in pyproject.toml keep test code productive without polluting global ignores. Trade-off: tests may drift from standards, but the ROI of annotating/documenting test helpers is near-zero.

C901 noqa with justification: when McCabe complexity 11-15 is inherent to the domain (CLI option handling, tree-sitter parsing, markdown extraction), add '# noqa: C901 -- complexity N, refactor deferred' inline. Splitting these functions would reduce readability. Document the count (32 functions) in audit results for future tracking.

Tiered violation fix strategy: (1) auto-fix first (ruff --fix), (2) global ignores for rules that conflict with project decisions (ANN401 when pyright strict handles it), (3) per-file ignores for entire directories (tests, protocols), (4) manual fixes for remaining. Process 247 violations in ~120 min by triaging before fixing.
PAT-E-023 PAT-E-024 PAT-E-025
yaml.safe_load() returns Any — after isinstance(raw, dict) check, use cast('dict[str, Any]', raw) for pyright strict compliance. Same pattern applies to all YAML loaders in the codebase.

When migrating test fixtures at scale (30+ occurrences), regex substitution is fragile. A line-by-line state machine (tracking normal/in_content/skip_body states) handles edge cases that regexes miss — especially nested delimiters and multi-line blocks in dedent().
God classes (>500 lines) are the highest-impact SRP violation. Decompose into loaders/formatters/data + orchestrator. The orchestrator becomes a thin pipeline importing from focused modules.
Silent except Exception hides real bugs. Every catch-all handler must log the exception at minimum debug level. Reinforced across 5 modules in E370 audit.
TDD RED phase reveals migration scope: count individual fixture occurrences (not just files). S370.5f estimated 3 files but touched 33 occurrences across 5 files.
Force-push rewrites all SHAs — unpushed local merges cause complete history divergence.

pipx install -e + pipx inject para dogfooding: mismo aislamiento que usuario final pero con source linkado. pipx no resuelve uv workspace sources — dependencias extras necesitan pipx inject explícito.
ACLI --json devuelve formato nested Jira API (fields.summary, fields.status.name). Write ops (create/edit/transition) devuelven envelope {results: [{id, status, message}]} donde id es el issue key.
Per-story MRs add overhead sin valor para solo dev. Per-epic MRs dan governance con menos fricción. Git log provee trazabilidad por story; MR provee trazabilidad por epic.
Pre-implementation AR for M+ stories catches dead code and simplification opportunities before writing code. Saved ~30 LOC and a config dependency in S494.3.
Interactive design with alternative comparison + 'what makes it PRO' question improves even XS stories. Validated in RAISE-594 — elevated from 6 inline lines to proper module with 3 guards.
Research grounding before design eliminates assumption risk — ACLI has no --server flag, discovered via 7-tool survey
Cross-reference validation: when config field A references field B by name, TDD must test B exists
health() methods must never raise — return unhealthy status, catch all internal errors
Deletion + entry point rewire must be one atomic commit
uv sync --all-extras for workspace package reinstall, not uv pip install --reinstall
Integration tests find what unit mocks hide — mocks validate assumptions, not contracts
ACLI response format map: create→full issue, edit→envelope, link→no --json
Sesiones de research libre pueden escalar de parking lot item a product definition completa si se permite exploración guiada con grounding progresivo
Lección Palantir: ontology es application-layer construct, no database feature. Moat = semantic model + SDK + flywheel, no storage engine
Para decisiones estructurales, lanzar 3+ agentes de research en paralelo cubriendo academia, industria y SOTA. La triangulación produce decisiones de alta confianza
Validate understanding against source of truth (README, SKILL.md) before accepting explanations

Official Atlassian Rovo MCP has severe token verbosity (24k tokens for trivial queries, 80k+ for edits). Community alternative sooperset/mcp-atlassian is more practical for daily use. aashari TOON format offers 30-60% token savings but uses generic HTTP tools.
Discovery del propio sistema requiere leer código fuente como fuente de verdad, no docs — el código revela la arquitectura real (17 NodeTypes, 11 EdgeTypes, 3 memory tiers)
Portability is distribution, not content: when the core artifact format (SKILL.md) is already cross-compatible, multi-platform support reduces to a path-mapping problem in the scaffolding layer

Verify claims against primary sources before synthesizing: initial research mixed .antigravity/ with .agent/, skills with workflows, .md with .toml — second pass against official docs corrected 4 factual errors
scaffold_skills() idempotency blocks upgrades — no mechanism to sync skills after pip upgrade. Version tracking exists in frontmatter but is not compared.
Pydantic v2 frozen models: use model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True), not class keyword frozen=True — pyright strict mode rejects the class keyword form

from __future__ import annotations breaks Typer runtime type inspection for custom types (Enum, Literal). Typer needs real types at runtime, not string annotations. Remove __future__ import from CLI command modules.
uv tool install creates a global install separate from project editable install. During development, uv run ensures project code is used. Bare command uses global tool.
Typer CliRunner resolves types differently than real CLI — tests can pass while actual CLI is broken. E2E tests via CliRunner give false confidence for type-resolution bugs.

Para validar documentación de API, ejecutar los endpoints reales durante la sesión — no solo leer el código. El comportamiento real (paths, auth errors, response shape) difiere de lo que sugiere el código sin ejecutar.
DualWriteBackend en rai-cli se activa solo con RAI_SERVER_URL + RAI_API_KEY en env vars. No requiere cambios de código — es feature flag por entorno. Útil para demo PRO sin modificar CLI.
Para demo de integración externa sin endpoints faltantes: mockear en la Forge action (return hardcodeado con TODO:fetch comentado), no en el server. El seam es limpio y el swap a real es una línea.

Para desarrollo de rai-cli: usar 'uv tool install --editable .' desde el repo. Crea venv aislado, apunta al source, git pull toma efecto inmediatamente. Verificar con: cat ~/.local/share/uv/tools/rai-cli/lib/python3.12/site-packages/_rai_cli.pth
Lint debt accumulates silently when an earlier gate (pytest) fails — fixing the first gate reveals all masked violations at once. Plan for a lint cleanup pass after fixing collection failures.

When pyright loses type after isinstance narrowing on dict[str, Any] value access (produces dict[Unknown, Unknown]), cast the reassignment explicitly: data = cast(dict[str, Any], data['key']). The isinstance guard makes the cast safe.
context: pyright typing cast isinstance dict narrowing

content: || true en jobs de CI enmascara fallos reales como verde. snyk:sast y snyk:ica aparecen green en la pipeline UI pero no produjeron reportes. Siempre leer el log del job, no confiar solo en el color del status.
RAISE-302 es más real que RAISE-303: cuando el input tainted es un componente dentro de un base dir (no el base mismo), path traversal es factible. Fix correcto: resolve() + is_relative_to(base).

Distinguir threat model antes de descartar SAST como FP: RAI_HOME como base dir es FP para CLI local. session_id como componente dentro de base dir es legítimo (CI puede inyectarlo). Mismo scanner, distinta severidad real.

Al integrar con servidores MCP, siempre verificar result.is_error antes de parsear. isError es contrato estándar del protocolo (Anthropic SDK CallToolResult). Ignorarlo produce salida vacía sin diagnóstico — el usuario no puede distinguir credenciales incorrectas, red caída, o bug.

Para diagnosticar bugs de adaptadores MCP sin instrumentar el CLI completo: script directo con McpBridge + export credentials. Más rápido que añadir logging al CLI y reproduce el raw content antes de cualquier parsing.

rai docs publish hace upsert automático: si el artifact_type tiene page_id en .raise/confluence-pages.yaml, llama confluence_update_page; si no, crea y guarda el ID. Auto-heal incluido para 404. El gap es que el --help no lo documenta.
Existing content in other repos should be audited before writing new docs — raise-gtm had 9 high-quality bilingual docs pages that only need migration and updating, not rewriting
Sync scripts that modify source files can introduce syntax errors (skills_base/__init__.py duplicated list assignment) — always run quality gates after sync

Verify subagent bulk transforms before committing — grep for expected changes and residual old values. Subagents doing find-and-replace across many files frequently miss edge cases (code blocks, headings, anchor links).
Verify subagent bulk transforms before committing — grep for expected changes and residual old values. Subagents doing find-and-replace across many files frequently miss edge cases.

CLI --help shows all commands including internal/PRO — use memory cli-reference.md as authoritative scope for public docs, not raw --help output. Core commands: init, session, memory, discover, skill, profile, base, release. PRO/internal: backlog, publish.

pattern: Emoji in regex needs raw string check alongside .lower() - Unicode doesnt lowercase emoji

content: Inconsistent defaults between paired commands (add/reinforce, write/read) create silent round-trip failures — both commands report success but operate on different files
Backlog management sessions surface architectural insights when you look at WHY data is inconsistent, not just WHAT to clean up. The graph-build-from-Jira gap led to the cartridge unification
Three parallel research agents covering different facets of the same question produce triangulated evidence in ~2 minutes wall-clock. 24 sources with HIGH confidence on all major claims
DDD Anti-Corruption Layer maps directly to adapter pattern: each cartridge adapter IS the ACL translating external schemas (Jira fields, Confluence pages, YAML files) into internal GraphNode model
Problem shaping before epic design catches orthogonal problems early (session identity vs workstream were conflated until brief process separated them)
E2E testing catches integration bugs that unit tests miss — close using legacy IDs instead of active pointer was invisible to mocked tests but immediately visible in E2E
Challenge 'shared by default' — moving data from gitignored to committed is a privacy change requiring explicit consent, not just a technical migration
Timestamp IDs eliminate coordination — no counters, no locks, no git pull needed. Each environment generates unique IDs independently
ActiveSessionPointer as typed JSON model — carrying metadata between lifecycle phases via a structured file is more reliable than reconstructing state
EnterWorktree creates from local HEAD, not remote — if local branch is stale, worktree is born stale. Always git pull dev before creating worktree
CI runs ruff format --check AND ruff check — local pre-commit must run both, not just linting
Never git add -A — it catches test artifacts, gitignored files, and unrelated changes. Always add specific files

Keep a Changelog entries should be manually curated from git log + Jira tickets for accuracy. Automated generation from conventional commits is a future optimization but manual curation catches context that commit messages miss.
v2.3.0 smoke test verification
Smoke tests for releases should verify each Added/Changed entry in the changelog, not just the original 3 core tests. Expanded coverage caught integration confidence that unit tests can't provide.
During release prep, verify each documentation deliverable's audience (Pro vs Community) before writing. Mixing audiences in docs creates confusion for users who can't access Pro features.

Logic+Code+Incorrect bugs (dead code, regex ambiguity, identical branches) accumulate silently and are always trivial to fix. The cost is detection latency, not fix complexity. SAST gate in CI eliminates the entire class.

After fixing a SAST/linter finding, re-run the specific rule that detected it before closing. Partial fixes create follow-up bugs (RAISE-536→RAISE-589: regex grouping fix was incomplete, needed second pass).

For security-critical transitive deps (jwt, crypto, tls), use workspace-level constraint-dependencies (uv) or npm overrides (JS) to force minimum versions. Individual package floors dont control transitives — CVEs hide in the dependency tree.
context: security cve dependency transitive uv-constraint npm-override

content: When N call sites share the same hardcoded assumption (encoding, format, magic string), the fix is a central module that encapsulates the assumption — not N individual patches. The first copy sets the pattern; every subsequent copy reinforces the debt.
context: cli encoding abstraction hardcoded symbols duplication

content: Package split migrations need an explicit checklist: entry points, dependencies, optional deps, scripts, CLI subcommands. Verify each package runs independently post-split. The monolith pyproject.toml handled everything implicitly — splitting exposes missing configs.
Before classifying a bug that names a component, grep git history to verify it exists/existed — components get deleted and bugs become orphaned
Fix only the reported bug — adding adjacent features (create_issue assignee, new CLI flags) during a bugfix is YAGNI. Scope discipline: RED test → minimal GREEN → done
